1: I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.
2: Shew me wherefore thou contendest with me.
3: And shine upon the counsel of the wicked?
4: Or seest thou as man seeth?
5: Are Are thy years as man's days,
7: And there is there is none that can deliver out of thine hand.
8: Together round about; yet thou dost destroy me.
9: And wilt thou bring me into dust again?
11: And hast fenced me with bones and sinews.
12: And thy visitation hath preserved my spirit.
13: I know that this is is with thee.
14: And thou wilt not acquit me from mine iniquity.
16: And again thou shewest thyself marvellous upon me.
17: Changes and war are are against me.
18: Oh that I had given up the ghost, and no eye had seen me!
19: I should have been carried from the womb to the grave.
20: And And let me alone, that I may take comfort a little,
21: Even Even to the land of darkness and the shadow of death;
22: And where where the light is is as darkness.
